segments.text: At T plus 0.678 seconds, the first warning appeared.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: It was smoke from the aft field joint of Challenger's right solid rocket booster.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: More smoke followed through the first two and a half seconds of flight.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Then it stopped.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: For the next minute, Challenger looked like a launch.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The vehicle climbed.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The call-outs continued.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The machinery of the mission appeared to be doing what it had done before.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: But the accident had already begun.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The visible catastrophe came later, about 73 seconds after liftoff.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: By then the public version could be told quickly.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: It was cold, the O-rings failed, and seven crew members died.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: That version is accurate.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: It is also too small to explain why launch was still allowed.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The smoke is the better starting point because it separates the consequence from the beginning.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: At 73 seconds, the chain became public.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: At less than 1 second, the chain became physical.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Before that, the chain had already been organizational.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The harder question starts before ignition.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: How does a warning stay visible in records, inspections, memos, charts, and meetings, then arrive at the final decision two weeks to stop the machine?
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Challenger did not fail because the danger was invisible.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: It failed after a system learned how to keep flying with evidence that should have made flying harder.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: To understand how that happens, start with the promise the shuttle system had to keep.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Mercury, Gemini, and Apollo were built around singular missions, dedicated hardware, dedicated risk, dedicated public attention.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The shuttle was designed to break that model.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Reusable, frequent, routine, less expedition, more airline.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: That promise shaped the organization around it.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: When a program promises routine, every delay starts to mean something different.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: A scrub is still an engineering decision, but it is also a schedule problem, a budget problem, a public problem, a problem that moves through contracts, payload windows, congressional expectations, and the reputation of the agency.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: NASA entered 1986 with an aggressive flight schedule and a system that had to justify itself through repetition.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The shuttle was not only a spacecraft, it was an operating model.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The first thing to understand about Challenger is that the program was built to make spaceflight feel normal.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Normal operation is useful.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: It is also dangerous when it teaches people to treat unresolved problems as background conditions.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: No one in this story needed to wake up wanting a disaster.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: That matters because the easy version turns the accident into a morality play.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Bad people made bad choices, so better people would have stopped it.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The record is harder than that.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The system was full of people trying to do their jobs inside a decision structure that kept lowering the force of warnings.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Three pressures were running at the same time.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: First, technical uncertainty.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The shuttle was enormously complex.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Each flight produced new evidence about how hardware behaved in the real vehicle under real loads in real weather.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: That gap between design expectation and operating evidence is normal in engineering.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: What matters is how the gap gets handled.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Second, schedule pressure.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Delays did not move one item on one calendar.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: They shifted payloads, crews, customers, reviews, and promises.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The cost of stopping was visible immediately.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Third, institutional reputation.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: NASA was not only an engineering organization.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: It was a national symbol, a funding case, a Cold War instrument, and a public trust.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: These pressures met inside the same rooms.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: When they conflicted, the most flexible thing was often uncertainty.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: A technical concern could be reframed.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: It could be qualified.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: It could be moved to the next review.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Schedule and reputation were harder to bend.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: So the practical standard drifted.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The question became, keep flying unless the evidence against flying is overwhelming.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: That is very different from, do not fly unless the evidence for safety is clear.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The difference showed up most clearly in one component.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The shuttle used two solid rocket boosters for most of its thrust during the first two minutes of flight.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Each booster was not built as one continuous piece.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: It was assembled from cylindrical segments, stacked and joined at the launch site.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: where the segments met, there was a field joint.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Inside that joint, two rubber O-rings, a primary and a secondary, were supposed to seal the gap and keep hot combustion gases inside the booster.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The joint had to seal quickly.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Pressure inside the motor could distort the metal parts at ignition.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The O-rings had to move into position fast enough to block hot gas before it escaped.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Cold mattered because rubber does not respond the same way in cold.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: A seal that moves quickly when warm can respond more slowly when cold.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: In this joint, timing was not a detail.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Timing was the safety margin.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: If the seals failed, there was no graceful backup.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Hot gas could burn through the booster casing, reach surrounding structure, and turn a local leak into a vehicle failure.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The o-ring problem had been showing itself before Challenger.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Post-flight inspections found erosion on the primary seal.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: In some cases, gas had blown past it and left evidence deeper in the joint.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Engineers documented the damage.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: They measured it.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: They reported it.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The dangerous part is what happened next.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Because the mission succeeded, because the vehicles came home, damage could be processed as evidence that the system had tolerated the condition.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: A damaged component in a surviving system can be read two ways.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: One reading says, we were closer to failure than we should have been, so we stop and fix the condition before we repeat it.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The other says, the vehicle survived, the margin held, and the risk can be carried while improvement work continues.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The second reading became easier each time the shuttle flew and returned.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: What had started as a serious anomaly gradually became part of the operating history.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The O-ring warning did not disappear.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: It moved.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: That is how a warning can die inside a large organization, not through silence, through translation.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: It starts when an engineer sees something wrong.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The data is specific.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The language is uncomfortable.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Erosion.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Blow by.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Heat effects.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: A seal that did not behave the way the design assumed.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Precise evidence has force.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: then the evidence becomes a discussion item.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: It enters a meeting with dozens of other concerns, other anomalies, other systems, other deadlines.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The context broadens and the urgency flattens.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: From there, the discussion becomes a judgment call.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Does this affect the next flight?
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The answer depends on the question the room decides to ask.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: If the question is, can we prove this will cause a failure?
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The answer is usually no.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Predicting a specific loss on a specific flight is hard.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: If the question is, can we prove this will not fail?
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The answer is also no.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: But the first question keeps the schedule alive.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Then the judgment becomes a schedule decision.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The language changes again.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: It is no longer mainly about erosion depth or sealing time.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: It is about whether there is enough cause to delay.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Delay has a phone tree.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Delay has cost.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Delay has a visible owner.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: If the flight succeeds, the decision becomes precedent.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: we flew with that condition and came back.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: That fact starts to compete with the warning that preceded it.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: This route ran more than once before Challenger.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Each pass made the next one easier to defend.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Engineers were not simply ignored, they were often heard, then absorbed into a process that had learned how to carry their concern without stopping.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Months before Challenger, Roger Boyzoli warned that a similar field joint problem could lead to catastrophe of the highest order and loss of human life.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: That memo did not come from hindsight.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: It came from inside the system, before the accident, from an engineer close to the hardware.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The temperature pattern also had more weight than the launch night argument made it feel.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: When the full flight history was framed one way, the cold weather signal could look debatable.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: When the full record was framed another way, cold O-ring distress became much harder to dismiss.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: A chart can contain real information and still weaken the warning if it frames the wrong question.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Richard Feynman's later appendix named the broader danger plainly.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Previous success can become misleading evidence.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: A survived flight can show that the vehicle got through.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: It does not prove the risk was safe to repeat.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: By January 1986, Challenger was not facing a new problem.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The program had evidence of o-ring distress.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: It had temperature concern.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: It had waivers.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: It had memos.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: It had a known field joint problem.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: It had a launch system that kept treating survival as part of the safety case.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Then the weather turned that old problem into a launch decision.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: January 27th, 1986.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Less than 24 hours before the scheduled launch.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: A teleconference connected NASA's Marshall Space Flight Center, Kennedy Space Center, and Morton Thyacol, the contractor that built the solid rocket boosters.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The subject was temperature.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Forecasts and engineering estimates pointed to launch conditions far colder than prior shuttle experience.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The launch temperature would be about 36 degrees Fahrenheit.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The right aft field joint was estimated colder, around 28 degrees Fahrenheit, plus or minus 5 degrees.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Theocal engineers were concerned because cold could slow the O-rings at the exact moment they needed to seal.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Their recommendation was clear.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Do not launch below 53 degrees Fahrenheit.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: That number mattered because it was tied to the lowest relevant prior experience they believed they could defend.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: NASA managers pushed back.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: In a technical review, pushback can be necessary.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The danger was the standard that began to govern the room.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The question drifted.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Show that this is safe to fly.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Became prove that it will fail.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: That shift reversed the burden of proof.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Instead of requiring evidence that launch was safe,
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The process demanded evidence that failure was certain.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Engineering evidence usually does not arrive that way.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: It arrives as a missing margin, a condition outside experience, a trend with enough signal to matter before the catastrophe supplies certainty.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The engineers had evidence of risk.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: They did not have an equation that converted temperature into guaranteed loss of vehicle.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: That absence became useful to the launch case.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Theakol asked for an offline caucus.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The caucus lasted far longer than the five minutes first requested.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: When they returned, the recommendation had changed.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The new position was that the data was inconclusive and launch could proceed.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The cold had not changed.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The joint had not changed.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The limits of the data had not changed.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The decision frame changed.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: One instruction from that caucus still carries the shape of the moment.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Jerry Mason told Bob Lund to take off his engineering hat and put on his management hat.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: No single phrase explains the disaster.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: No single person explains it either.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The deeper fact is that the same process could take a specific engineering objection and return a launch recommendation from the same evidence.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The launch eve reversal is dramatic, but it is not the whole failure.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: A warning also has to travel.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: It has to move from hardware to engineers, from engineers to managers, from contractors to NASA, from lower review levels to the people with authority to stop the launch.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: At each step, the signal can be compressed.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Compression is normal in large programs, but when compression changes an active objection into a resolved item, the system has made a decision without naming it directly.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Safety should have been a backstop.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The Commission later criticized weak problem reporting, inadequate trend analysis, misrepresented criticality, and safety structures that did not give the concern enough independent force at the decisive points.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: People can care about safety while the way decisions are made still lets a warning arrive too late, too thin, or too resolved to change action.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: By launch morning, the warning had passed through hardware evidence, paperwork, meetings, waivers, charts, recommendation, caucus and management acceptance.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: At the end of that route, launch was still open.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Eastern Standard Time on January 28, 1986, Challenger launched from Kennedy Space Center.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Within the first second, smoke appeared near the right aft field joint.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The primary o-ring had not sealed the way it needed to seal.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The secondary o-ring, affected by cold and joint movement, had not saved the joint in time.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The smoke continued through roughly T plus two and a half seconds.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Then it disappeared.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The commission considered that combustion residue may have temporarily sealed the breach.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: For a short time, the vehicle could look normal while the failure chain remained alive.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: That temporary quiet is one of the clearest details in the record.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The physical system did what the organization had been doing for years.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: A sign of breach appeared, an explanation arrived, and flight continued.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Around 58 to 59 seconds into flight, flame became visible again.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Hot gas from the right booster began damaging surrounding structure, including the external tank area.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: At approximately 73 seconds, Challenger broke apart.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Francis Dick Scoby, Michael Smith, Judith Resnick, Ellison Onizuka, Ronald McNair, Gregory Jarvis, and Krista McAuliffe were lost.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The visible failure lasted seconds.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The invisible one had been accumulating for years.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: What came next was the question every institution fears.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: How did this happen when the evidence already existed?
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: President Reagan appointed the Rogers Commission, chaired by William Rogers.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Richard Feynman was one of its members, and his independent work became part of the public memory of the investigation.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The physical cause was direct, failure of the seal in the right booster's aft field joint, hot gas escaping, damage to the external tank area, and vehicle breakup.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The commission did not stop at the physical cause,
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: It found a flawed launch decision process.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: It found communication failures.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: It found that crucial information about O-ring history, temperature concern, and continuing engineering opposition did not reach key decision makers with the force and clarity it needed.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The people closest to the hardware had the sharpest version of the risk.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The people with the authority to stop launch received a softened version of it.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The commission described the immediate failure, the part, the launched decision, and the communication system that softened the warning.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Diane Vaughan later named the larger pattern, the normalization of deviance.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: A deviation from expected performance becomes normal because the system keeps surviving it.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The phrase is useful because it does not require a conspiracy.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: It describes a slow adjustment in what feels acceptable.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The line moves because each previous crossing did not end in catastrophe.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Challenger is taught in engineering programs, business schools, and organizational behavior courses because the pattern is not limited to NASA.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Repeated near misses create false confidence.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Every time a system survives a close call, perceived risk can drop.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The near miss gets counted as validation instead of warning.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Missing data gets mistaken for safety.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: No evidence of failure is not the same as evidence of no failure, especially when the dangerous condition has not really been tested.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: schedule pressure changes proof standards quietly no one has to announce that the bar has moved the bar for stopping can rise inch by inch until only certainty of disaster justifies delay.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Institutions reward continuity over interruption.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The person who keeps the line moving is easier to celebrate than the person who stops it for a risk that might never become visible.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Anomalies become background noise.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The first time something unexpected happens, it gets attention.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The fifth time, a tracking number.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The tenth time, it starts to sound like how the system works.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: These behaviors appear wherever the cost of stopping is immediate, and the cost of continuing is statistical.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Healthcare systems, financial systems, infrastructure, software.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: any organization that has to decide whether a warning is strong enough to interrupt momentum.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Challenger did not invent this pattern.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: It made the pattern visible.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Disasters rarely begin at the moment of impact.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: They begin when a system gets used to explaining away what it should have stopped to understand.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Challenger was a long sequence of accommodations.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Each one easier to defend than the whole chain would have been.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The smoke at T plus 0.678 seconds is why the story should not start with the explosion.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The first visible warning was small.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Then it vanished.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: For a while, everything looked normal.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The same thing had already happened inside the organization.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: A warning appeared.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The system explained it.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The launch continued.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: That is why the next case belongs beside Challenger.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Therak 25 is not another space story.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: It is a smaller room, a medical machine, a software interface, and operators trying to explain errors the machine was not built to make visible.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The form changes.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The pattern does not.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Confidence moves faster than verification.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Next time.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Therac 25.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: A medical machine that exposed what happens when software confidence outruns the evidence users can see.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00
